free_text: Formation cross sections of various radionuclides from
Ni,Fe,Si,Mg,O and C for protons of energies between
130 and 400 MeV.
EN-RSL - energy spread
1-mil(25 micron) aluminium foil.
Na-24 activity was measured from the monitor
foil, using an end-window proportional counter.
In all cases, duplicate determinations
agreed within 5-12.%
Chemical separation method.
Some corrections for monitor and en-err.
Some cosmetic corrections.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Major corrections in all Subents.
SD: Corrections in Subents 008, 015, 018,
022-024. Subent 019 deleted (=O0073.020 divided by the
isotopic abundance of 25Mg and 26Mg (~0.21)).
/By authors/ Cross sections based on 96.18% of the
weight of iron.
Iron metal foil of 40 mg/cm**2 thickness was used.
Chemical separation was started 2 to 3 days
after the irradiation.
A methane flow-window proportional counter.
The total estimated uncertainties include
the errors and uncertainties involved in counting,
chemical yield determination, beam monitoring and
saturation factor.
Higher uncertainties are estimated for results at
130 MeV, because of low beam current.
Tbl. III from Canad.J.Chem.,42(1964)1149
SD: SF5=IND and SF9=EXP deleted from
REACTION code. BIB updated. EN-ERR -> EN-RSL.
Iron metal foil of 40 mg/cm**2 thickness was used.
Chemical separation was started 2 to 3 days
after the irradiation.
2 in. x 2 in. NaI(Tl) scintillation crystal in
conjunction with a 512-channel pulse-height analyzer.
The total estimated uncertainties include
the errors and uncertainties involved in counting,
chemical yield determination, beam monitoring and
saturation factor.
Higher uncertainties are estimated for results at
130 MeV, because of low beam current.

/By authors/ The cross sections may be considered as
upper limits for independent formation,since 30% of the
6.4 day Ni-56 had decayed by the time of chemical
separation.

Chemical separation was started 2 to 3 days
after the irradiation.
A methane flow-window proportional counter.
